headline: Malaysian Students Rank 45th in Global Financial Literacy Assessment
context: OECD's 2025 financial literacy assessment placed Malaysian 15-year-olds well below the global average.

cards.big: Malaysian students ranked 45th of 58 countries in 2025 OECD financial literacy test
cards.sub: Only 28% of students demonstrated basic budgeting and saving competency

cards.big: Financial education is embedded in 3 existing subjects but has no dedicated curriculum time
cards.sub: Fragmented delivery across math life skills and moral education dilutes learning

cards.big: AKPK data shows average Malaysian household debt-to-income ratio at 86% in 2025
cards.sub: Adult financial vulnerability traces back to inadequate financial education in schools

cards.big: Australia's MoneySmart program achieved 34% improvement in student financial literacy since 2018
cards.sub: Dedicated curriculum with real-world simulations produces measurable competency gains

cards.big: Students who cannot manage money at 15 become adults who cannot manage debt at 30
Should financial literacy become a standalone required subject in Malaysian secondary schools
